type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This is due to the increased digester biogas flared this year (12,804,440 m3) compared to the last year (10,793,387 m3).

This year, none of the total digester gas produced at the plant was utilized in high-efficiency co-generators engines. The remainder of the digester gas (unscrubbed) is burned off at the flare building. Thus all the digester gas produced at Bonnybrook is burned in one way or the other, which converts H2S contained in the digester gas into SO2, and subsequently the SO2 is released to the atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 12,804,440 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v), 
H2S content in the scrubbed digester gas = 0.005% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Emission Factor “1.066 kgVOCs / 1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. The Bonnybrook plant uses anaerobic digestion process and the VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with the digester gas in the co-generation system. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
The Digester Gas is burned as a fuel in the engines to generate electricity. The remainder digester gas is burned off at the flare building. Thus all the digester gas produced at Bonnybrook is burned in one way or the other, which converts H2S contained in the digester gas into SO2, and subsequently the SO2 released to the atmosphere.

Total digester gas production in the plant = 13,676,971 m3
Typical H2S content in the digester gas = 0.20% (v/v)

When H2S is burned, stoichiometrically 34 tonnes of H2S is converted into 64 tonnes of SO2.

(from 2010’s 15,626 dry tonnes @ 3.303 mg As /kg of dry solids to 18,155 dry tonnes @ 3.45 mg As/kg of dry solids). type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
Emission Factor “1.066 kg VOCs/1000 m3 wastewater processed" was used to determine the Total VOCs air-emission. Bonnybrook Plant uses anaerobic digestion process and the VOCs contained in the digester gas are burned off along with digester gas in the co-generation system. Using the emission factors available from FIRE 6.25 database, these individual VOC compounds emitted from digesters were estimated and then subtracted from ‘Total VOCs’ in order to estimate the net VOCs emission to the atmosphere.

Although the plant met 10-tonne Total VOCs threshold under Part 4, our VOCs air-emission model based on the Emission Factors (from EPA FIRE 6.25 database) determined that none of the 75 speciated VOCs individually met the 1-tonne threshold criterion for the Part 5 Substances reporting.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: This WWTP removes Ammonia/Nitrogen to meet the effluent quality required in our Provincial and Federal regulations. In addition to having no control over the influent (quality or quantity), Ammonia/Nitrogen concentration and volume in the effluent pathways (air, plant effluent and biosolids) varies each year due to population changes, industrial activity, and plant performance. As a result, the quantity varies each year.
